_note: ORIGINAL INPUT-CONTROL TEST, done right (persistent struct write). The unit poller writes evade INPUT bytes on each tracked unit's LIVE battle struct every ~20ms, BEFORE the VM avoidance roll reads them. This is exactly the original idea: change memory before Denuvo calculates. We set ALL five evade bytes to 100 (max) on ALL units (TargetCharId=-1): weapon evade +0x46/+0x47, shield evade +0x4A/+0x4E, class evade +0x4B. We never need to identify the defender at the roll moment - we persistently arm the struct and let the unit be attacked. RUN: relaunch the game (new DLL has the override). Then ATTACK AN ENEMY FROM THE FRONT with a physical melee (evade applies from front/side, NOT from behind). OBSERVE TWO SIGNALS: (1) the hit% FORECAST shown when you target it - did it drop far below the normal ~100% front hit? (2) execute the attack - did it MISS / did the target play a dodge/block/parry animation? PASS (input-control viable) = forecast drops AND/OR the attack misses; the [SELECTOR-PROBE] log shows evadeType != 0x00 (0x02 weapon parry=+46/47, 0x03 shield block=+4A/4E, 0x04 class evade=+4B - tells us which byte the VM honored). FAIL (VM read a cached copy) = forecast stays ~100% AND the attack hits normally with evadeType=0x00 despite [EVADE-OVERRIDE] confirming the bytes were written to 0x64. The [EVADE-OVERRIDE] lines confirm the live writes landed. Safe: struct writes only, no result forging.
